steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage gives two specific examples (Wisconsin 1983, Vermont 1985) showing years elapsed between selection and designation. The blank must introduce a general conclusion drawn from those examples — a frequently-true pattern, not a universal law.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In many cases,: signals a frequent but not universal pattern, correctly framing the generalization the examples support. ✓
  • Choice B — Therefore,: signals a logically necessary conclusion; the examples are illustrations, not premises that force a deductive conclusion. ✗
  • Choice C — Indeed,: signals confirmation of a claim already stated; no such general claim has been made before this sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — Similarly,: signals a parallel comparison between two items; there is only one claim here, not two parallel ones. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
